title: Transaction Logs for Digital Product Sales Explained for Digital Creators: The Honest Answer
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling ebooks, courses, templates, fonts, and digital downloads with automated delivery and crypto payments — no platform middleman required.

content.text: You’re a digital creator in Nigeria, Kenya, or Pakistan. You’ve got an ebook, course, or template ready to sell. But PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad block you at every turn. Even if you get past KYC, your buyers can’t pay. Sound familiar? Here’s the honest truth: every sale you make needs a record. Not just for taxes, but to prove what happened when a buyer claims they never got their download. Transaction logs are that record. This post breaks down what they are, how they work, and why they’re non-negotiable for selling digital products in restricted markets.
What This Actually Is and Why It Matters

A transaction log is a timestamped record of every sale. It includes the buyer’s payment details, the product they purchased, and the delivery status. Think of it as a receipt for both you and the buyer, but stored securely in your system. For digital creators in countries like Iran, Venezuela, or Bangladesh, this log is your proof when a buyer disputes a charge or claims they never received their ebook or template. It’s also how you track revenue, spot trends, and manage refunds without relying on a third-party platform. Without it, you’re flying blind—and vulnerable.
How the Technology Works Under the Hood

When a buyer pays for your digital product, the transaction log is created automatically. It captures the payment method (crypto wallet, BTC, ETH, etc.), the amount in USD (converted at transaction time), and the product ID. This data is stored in a secure database linked to your store. For example, if you’re selling a font pack in the Philippines and a buyer pays with USDT, the log records the wallet address, the USD equivalent, and the font pack’s unique ID. When the file is delivered, the log updates to mark the order as complete. All this happens without you lifting a finger.
Who This Is Built For and Why

This system is for creators who can’t use mainstream platforms due to geography, banking, or KYC restrictions. If you’re selling ebooks in Ethiopia, courses in Tanzania, or beats in Cameroon, transaction logs are your safety net. They’re also for solo developers building crypto-native stores who need transparency without middlemen. The logs ensure you can operate globally, accept 300+ coins, and still keep track of every sale. No more relying on PayPal’s approval or Stripe’s fees. You control the data, the delivery, and the proof.
Automated delivery means the download link arrives before the buyer navigates away from the payment confirmation page.
The Components That Make It Work Together

Three components power this system: the payment processor (NOWPayments), the delivery engine (Core Shop Engine, $49.99), and the transaction log itself (part of the Admin Dashboard, $29.99). NOWPayments handles the crypto payments, the Core Shop Engine automates delivery, and the Admin Dashboard stores and displays the logs. Together, they create a seamless, automated system. For example, when a buyer in Pakistan purchases your Notion template with SOL, NOWPayments processes the payment, the Core Shop Engine sends the file, and the Admin Dashboard logs the entire transaction. It’s fully automated, global, and KYC-free.
How to Use This Knowledge to Take Action

Start by setting up a store that includes transaction logs. Use the Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) to get everything in one package: payment processing, automated delivery, and the Admin Dashboard for logs. Once your store is live, every sale is logged automatically. Use the logs to track revenue, manage disputes, and optimize your product offerings. For example, if you notice a spike in sales for a specific preset in Kenya, you can create more products like it. Transaction logs aren’t just a record—they’re your roadmap to scaling your digital product business in restricted markets.

Your Action Checklist

Prepare your product files in delivery-ready format — PDF for ebooks, ZIP archives for multi-file products like font families or preset packs. Set your prices in USD and use NOWPayments to handle the crypto equivalent calculation automatically. Deploy the Core Shop Engine and configure your product list with file paths, prices, and delivery settings. Set up Web3 Wallet Connect with your product display and checkout configuration. Test the checkout flow in a browser on both desktop and mobile — confirm the QR code generates correctly and the payment amount displays accurately. Complete a real mainnet test transaction and confirm the delivery email arrives with a working download link. Open the Admin Dashboard and verify the test transaction appears with correct details. Once the test passes, publish your store URL across your content channels. Review your first week of transactions in the Admin Dashboard to confirm everything is running correctly.
No platform middleman means no percentage cut, no payout delay, and no content policy that can delist your products.
Admin Dashboard — $29.99. Transaction logs, order management, and real-time analytics. See every order, every payment, every delivery — from anywhere in the world.
